parameters.content:
### Daily Schedule

Starts the workflow on a timer — daily at 9:00. No input data; downstream nodes fetch what they need.

Next: Read Pending Queue (Airtable)

### Read Pending Queue (Airtable)

airtable — `search` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Normalize Booking Payload · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Airtable PAT

### Normalize Booking Payload

Custom JavaScript: Normalize incoming booking records to a stable schema

Next: Work in Batches

### Work in Batches

Loop controller — releases 5 item(s) per pass. Output 2 (loop) processes the batch, output 1 (done) fires after the last batch.

Ports: ①→ Aggregate Batch Results · ②→ Process Batch Item

### Process Batch Item

POST https://api.example.com/v1/bookings — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Respect Rate Limits · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Respect Rate Limits

Pauses 2 seconds — spacing calls to respect rate limits.

Next: Work in Batches

### Aggregate Batch Results

Collapses all items into a single summary item (for counts and run stats).

Next: Mark Completed in Google Sheets

### Mark Completed in Google Sheets

googleSheets — `appendOrUpdate` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Save Execution Metadata · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

nodes.name: Note: Mark Completed in Google Sheets

parameters.content:
### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Cap Batch Volume

### Cap Batch Volume

Caps the stream at 100 items to protect downstream quotas.

Next: Send Summary to Slack

### Send Summary to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Slack

### Escalate Failure to Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

# Remind open deals with Airtable and Outlook end to end

**Pattern:** Reminders & Follow-ups · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Scheduled

## What it does
Remind open deals with Airtable and Outlook end to end. It runs automatically on a schedule.

## Why it exists
Manual calendar sync is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Daily Schedule → Read Pending Queue (Airtable) → Normalize Booking Payload → Work in Batches → Aggregate Batch Results → Mark Completed in Google Sheets → Save Execution Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to reminders & follow-ups without manual effort
- Anyone automating calendar sync in healthcare
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Responds in seconds
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Airtable PAT; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ⏱ Rate limiting

Work is batched and spaced with Wait so provider quotas aren't tripped. If you hit 429s, lower the batch size or raise the wait — throughput drops, reliability rises.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your calendar sync process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
